title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The PETS Act aims to ban the sale of dogs and cats in Massachusetts pet shops to curb puppy mill sales.
The bill encourages collaboration between pet stores and shelters to promote adoptions and responsible pet ownership.
Provisions also target housing and insurance barriers, ensuring broader access to pets in state-aided public housing.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Chanté Joseph explores why some women choose not to showcase their boyfriends on social media.
Joseph's viral essay suggests women may want to distance themselves from traditional heterosexual models.
The topic raises discussions about women's autonomy and societal expectations of relationships.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O'Neill, of Boston, is running the Boston Marathon supporting The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp.
The camp provides children with serious medical conditions a safe place for fun and support at no charge.
O'Neill's fiancée's sister, a cancer survivor and former camper, now serves as a camp counselor.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Philadelphia Eagles will not trade wide receiver A.J. Brown to the New England Patriots at this time.
Trade talks between the Patriots and Eagles may resume closer to June 1 due to salary cap considerations.
The Patriots previously offered first- and third-round draft picks for Brown, which the Eagles declined.

title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum will return to the court for the Celtics against the Wizards on Saturday after resting his right Achilles.
Tatum, playing under a minutes restriction, sat out Thursday's game as part of injury management after three consecutive starts.
Celtics' injury report: Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are probable, while Nikola Vucevic remains out with a finger fracture.

title: 5 things to know about Patriots offensive lineman Alijah Vera-Tucker
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots signed former Jets guard Alijah Vera-Tucker to strengthen offensive line after 68 sacks last season.
Vera-Tucker, age 26, offers versatility with starts at each line position except center, and recovers from recent injuries.
He joined Patriots with influence from former teammate Morgan Moses and values representing his Mexican heritage.

title: Alex Cora identified 2 players he views as leaders of the 2026 Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Manager Alex Cora names Trevor Story and Garrett Crochet as key leaders for the 2026 Red Sox.
Trevor Story, entering his 11th MLB season, played 157 games and led the team in several batting categories in 2025.
Garrett Crochet, noted for his strong pitching, led MLB in strikeouts in 2025 and is compared to top pitchers.

title: 4 things to watch during Boston Legacy FC’s NWSL debut against Gotham FC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC makes its NWSL debut against Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium on March 14, 2026.
Head coach Filipa Patão aims to implement a possession-style, attacking soccer despite having a majority of players new to NWSL.
Newly-installed grass field at Gillette Stadium may complement Legacy FC's desired tactical approach in Saturday's match.

title: Should Massachusetts stop changing its clocks?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers are debating a bill (S.2157) to end the twice-a-year clock change.
Senate President Karen E. Spilka supports the bill to establish a permanent time system in Massachusetts.
Research links clock changes to health issues like heart attacks, strokes, and circadian rhythm disruptions.

title: Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston Pride For The People announces the 2026 Pride celebration for June 6 with a parade and festival.
The parade starts at 11 a.m. at Copley Square, ending with a noon to 6 p.m. concert at Boston Common.
Mayor Michelle Wu praised the inclusive efforts and anticipates welcoming visitors from across the region and country.
